Now I'm going to transition to the last part of this training and this is where I'm going to show you guys examples of lead funnels. And this is what I'm so excited for. I found over 106 cool ones to share. There are a lot more but I want to show just my favorite. So the ones that like the page layout, like the design, like the coloring, like the lead magnet, things like that. All of them have different kind of reasons why I showed them. So I'm going to go through them all and I won't spend like 12 hours doing this, don't worry. I'm going to point out what I like about it and why I like about it so you guys can actually see those things and hopefully give you guys ideas. If you're looking at, I want you guys to look for ideas for a couple of things. Number one, look for ideas from design. Look at how these things are laid out. I picked them because they're simple designs. I like them. There's ones that I'm guessing the most are convert really, really high because people are buying a lot of paid ads to them. Number two, look for like what's the hook? What's the reason why someone's giving the email address? And number three is like, what's the lead magnet? What was that? That was super easy or simple or hard. As we break these down, you also will see like what's the funnel they're pushing people into after they opt in. So there's three segments I'm going to talk about here. The first one we're going to talk about are lead magnet layouts. The second then are going to be lead magnets to nurture people, to build a relationship, and the third are lead magnets to push into a cell. Are you excited for this? I'm excited. One thing he talked a lot about was he called the deer and the headlights effect. So that when a subscriber, new person's coming to you, it's kind of like a deer and they're coming and they're coming to get a drink of water and they're seeing this thing, but then they're like, they're nervous and they're kind of jittery and they're waiting. And if you like, if you step on a stick and they freak out and they run away, right? It's that all the other versions of landing pages they had, they had so much stuff on it that it was like, they, people would just leave and then this one was so simple that like it was like the, the deers that would come to the water and drink and then come into your funnel.
